insight.summary: Pydantic bytes fields silently fail JSON serialization for binary content
insight.detail: Pydantic v2 `bytes` fields serialize text-like content to JSON fine but raise `PydanticSerializationError: invalid utf-8 sequence` for actual binary data. This surfaces only when `model_dump_json()` is called with binary content — validation and dict operations work fine. Use `pydantic.Base64Bytes` instead, which base64-encodes on serialization and decodes on deserialization. Note that `Base64Bytes` requires base64-encoded string input even from Python — raw `b"hello"` raises a base64 decoding error.
insight.action: Use `pydantic.Base64Bytes` instead of `bytes` for any Pydantic field that may contain binary data and needs to round-trip through JSON.
